decks.author: lizzy
decks.notes:
phouse mines
26-2 to Master on day 1

Gameplan: play powerhouse with full hand, get that -14 cost discount, play a bunch of now-cheap cards that replace themselves. often you do nothing on t1-t3 so you can play t4 powerhouse with full hand. noelle is 6g powerhouse on a stick. remember you can use blitz quiz without having something to remove if you want to turn on punishment/noelle/snowdin sign/head hathy.

skris is antifatigue

taking damage is actually often a good thing against control/stall decks so that your passive and true justice will ping their face whenever they try to heal themselves -  multi shot, especially with lab sign, tends to deal a TON of face damage if your opponent has more hp

be careful to not draw all your powerhouses with zap cannon before playing noelle

also remember to generate spells that play nice with lab sign as opposed to like... ones that dont lol

Totem Patience 25-6 to early Onyx
I think this is the best version I've been able to make. The idea behind this deck is cost reduction fun combined with a pretty good draw engine. Lancer cold winter is also funny bit situational. decks.author: cow2970
decks.notes:
Aggro Bravery: Onyx IV-Legend

guess who's back

that's right it's meeee

15-5 according to the history but i won a few other games outside of it so really the record is more 20-7

credits to Apex Overlord for piquing my interest enough to pick up this (non-snail non-chargespam) variant

fairly simple mulligan: look for charles. charles is love, charles is life. most justices are of the "pass first three turns" variety so being able to develop a 3/1 that is immune to its passive is a godsend (unless, of course, they topdeck blitz quiz, in which case rest in rip). if you think you will need a more explosive follow-up you can keep a duck off, which you'll want to play on curve with program up if you follow the usual aggro philosophies. 

game plan is to develop resilient threats early, swing face, and finish off with ice craps and tems. always count your charges and keep your outs in mind. 

kindness is a worse mu than justice fsr, probably because the kindness decks i ran into tend to run anti-aggro 💀

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
burnteg
20-0 this specific exact version played diamond to master
this deck is hard to explain the playstyle of honestly, there is so many nuances i picked up playing it that i dont feel like explaining it all
but the bottom line is that you want to abuse integ passive with your small cycles early game, so always hard mull for maus / butterflies / cheese block, keeping kris or biker is also fine usually
in general planning out your curve to have the most integ procs while putting the most pressure on the opponent is usually the name of the game here
except for the fact if you cannot set up carousels then this deck will suck for you, you need to be good at setting them up and recognizing when it's value

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
royal paps integ

diamond-legend

Real winrate is a bit higher because this includes a slightly worse build, probably take away like 1-2 losses





I'll actually provide strategy tips on this since it's clearly in a category of decks that I call "competent meme"

1.Do not play this deck. It's incredibly clunky and the optimal way to play it is probably focus less on royal paps and add a bit more proactive and/or cheaper drops into this. The deck also can use Golden Hits. However my aim was to make an rpaps deck.

2.Always mulligan for Fortune. Your second priority mulligan is Onionsan since it's your only proactive drop (bar Undyne).

3.You can skip turn3 as player 2 as long as you think EX isn't coming. This will let you play big threat t4 + follow up swing t5. If you play big threat t3, opponent responds, you can be forced to skip t4 and you or your remaining board will take damage from the responding card.

4.Ex is nasty but not the end of the world if the enemy cannot pop your gifts. Gifts often let you come back from an ex since you get 3 additional gold. Yes, 3, because integrity passive will count the gold ex stole from you as "spent" and the passive will trigger even if you don't actually fulfill it after the ex.

5.Count your carousels. Without one, Asgore can easily turn into a dead card, and since this deck has no cycle, you really don't want dead cards. Mad Dummy can help as a last resort there. It's actually the weakest card in the deck, but I still prefer it over Jevil, though whether that's right of me is very arguable.

6.Contrary to popular belief, if you use Ugly Fish on a monster that costs less than 28, your ass will not spontaneously light on fire and kill you. Please.

7.Always think at least 1 turn ahead.

decks.author: wiza0
decks.notes:
Ambushvery 88-27 up to top 10
Could honestly go higher but I got -19 from one of these Diamond players and decided top 3 is a massive waste of time and energy :pain: 
Anyways as for the actual deck, your main goal is to bum rush noelle proc as soon as possible, use your Create a Machines for Flame of Love for the 7/4 dodge, then setup with certif + 1 cost, or just whatever is available in your hand.
Certif especially here is vile, pulling Queen, Elim Duck, or Fox Bodyguard will set you up pretty well. In the case your Noelle botdecks raw ambush is pretty good by itself, just make sure you are trying to get your t3 recruit so you get full value if that's the case. T3 is the highroll but T4/T5 are pretty consistent as long as you know what to look for.
Also the aggro matchup here is aids, you have no heals so you just gotta pray you can taunt wall them while you wait for your combo to show up. 
I run a couple one-offs here but those are just personal preference, optimal builds would probably swap out for either low-curve heals or more cycling

decks.author: dracokidyt
decks.notes:
Noice deck, get ball dancer ggwp

balldancer-justice

Played to legend from diamond
this specific build is 26-3 one loss because i played badly against pixel sign

- Ultima can be replaced with kris but i found more success with ultima, unironincally that candy helped alot on defense <:hue:1137287556757721178> 
This deck does take some time to get used to so you can play it optimally, a single mistake will most likely give you an instant loss, playstyle differs from matchups but against any deck in existence you should ALWAYS full mulligan to get ball dancer, a T2/T3 Ball dancer is really important for this deck, you can win even when botdecking ball dancer but its harder

- Your best turns should be at turn 6-7 and turn 12-13, you should aim to win the game as fast as possible but if you're against a stall deck you can easily burst them down with a big charge push at turn 13

- also for the love of god do NOT play this deck passively or wait for a big charge push, you should always keep the pressure and control the board using fly and revolt because your opponent can easily just summon big monsters to kill you with them. there ARE situations where you can stall for 1 turn or 2 to get a big charge push like when your opponent summons a full board that you can face tank

- you 100% can and should use your charges for defense, this can be played as control if you needed to, because you literally just need 3-4 charges so you can 30 to 0 your opponent in 1 turn, dont play this as braindead aggro must go face ooga booga

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Overgrowth-Mid-Bravery
36/6 aka 86% wr (tho real wr is higher because multiple accounts, source: trust me bro)
Played to 2280

A combination of my recent dabblings into overgrowth and old fishvery

Gameplan: you have the tools to consistently win earlygame exchanges and snowball them into a midgame victory. Some common openings with this deck are recruit drop into rage, froggit trio overgrowth (hitting mettabot and recruit pick as well), berd/clam girl and in case of a big enemy opener mettabot + susie or ugly fish. During mulligan you should keep these in mind and pick and choose depending on the matchup. Duck off (and by extension noelle) and heroine serve as your way to close the game out, keep in mind that you don’t have burst outside of random recruit drops so you gotta hit face whenever possible. 

Red bird is the 25th slot here but definitely worth it because it plays off your opponents perception of threat, allowing you to get enormous value from it (for example an opponent is very likely to ignore an ugly fish or damaged broken car in favor of building a board)

6/10 braincells required, mainly good mulligan and trading awareness

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
"Merc-Hire-Madness"

This is merc hire! Do you miss old integ with sillystrings spam and 23 good cards being viable? NOW YOU CAN DO THAT AND MORE!!!! 22 good cards and 1 dead card equal a whole fun value control stall mish mash of wacky stuff.

18-2

This deck was changed once.

2x Carn tent -> 1x Carn tent + Captn Rouxls

2x Polibear -> 2x Tall Sink (Yes, actually)

How to play:

Play control value integ. Try and play on curve if you can on t3-4 and then try and just go into control mode and keep doing the most value based lines (unless your setting up to die or are gonna die). Bookshelf is usually a dead card but then again, you only need 1 if you want near infinate value. Carn tent is... bad. So just dream it away so we never need to look at this atrocity again.

Played from D2 -> ONYX 1 (so close)

The combo: 

Have either a full board or 1 other monster on the far left.

Bookshelf -> Merc hire -> Other monster far left -> Carn Tent

"But im dying to Everyman Eye!!!!"

Simple: Get 2 shelfs and Tent

Play Shelf -> Cloning -> Clone shelf -> Play 2nd Shelf -> Merc hire -> Carn tent!

You now have 3 one cost shelfs and a 1 cost tent. (Mill is pretty irrelevant at this point)

Next turn:

Shelf×2 -> Silly Strings -> Shelf-> Merc hire -> Tent

You have now, Killed 2 monsters outright, created a board, presented and infinate loop, and gained +2 Anti.

You can get board locked but you still have 13 DMG which is a clock, not fast but they do need to answer it. 

Loses:

- Good tempo kindness where I died to not enough healing vs 12 burst. (The game made me change the deck up)

- Nearly perfect Libness draws and they never even played a lib. So I lost cuz insane tempo and always being behind from t3 onwards.
You can usually stabilize most the time but kindness is quite hard to

I really liked going back to a kind of old integ with ridiculous value and loops for random BS.

But this will need to be changed. This is dumb and way to easy to abuse lol.

decks.author: 4reis
decks.notes:
acidic-casdyne-giga-otk 
(original deck by <@971207396183650354> but i tried to find a way to adapt/counter to ⁠six-hp-lol users)

As I am writing this, I am in Legend with 2255 elo. #2 currently. 

I have played nothing but patience giga queen so far in ranked.

My winrate is a 70% right now because I was playing previous versions of this deck.

Giga Queen deck nerf please I beg. It's so fun to play otk vs stall but this is straight up unbalanced. -30 health in a singular turn with Casual Undyne hitting once and then using two Giga Attacks afterwards?

For players trying to read this and have no experience whatsoever playing Giga Queen, she requires 7 TOKENcards, which I will be calling them as Green Cards because it's much simpler to identify something by the color instead of using the word "Token". This is due to the stereotype that your average Undercards player cannot read huge walls of text.

Playstyle is dependent on matchup. Please do not take Giga Queen, Casual Undyne or Receptionist 3 as part of the 3 cards in your starting hand. It is never a good idea to have heavy cards in your starting hand versus aggro/zoo, you'd want them later. 

Try to get Giga Queen before turn 10 though when you've played 5 other green cards for it. 

Your deck does not need to calculate 7 green cards, but 5 instead because:

1. You will be playing this deck in Patience. Change of Winds is a green card.

2. You have Ambition as one of your artifacts. Dream is a green card.

7-2 is 5. I'm sure by now if you're reading this you know what basic math is.

The level of this deck is Intermediate-Advanced. 


Quality of Life Change for lazy people:


**Great cards to have before and during turn 5:**
**Tem Egg, Maus, Bag of Goodies, Memorial Statue, Purple Mascot, Cracked Table, Policeblook, Rudolph, Jar of Worms, Traffic Light.**

**Cards you would want by turn 10:**
**Giga Queen, Casual Undyne, Receptionist 3** (if you want).

**Tips for Traffic Light:**
Please remove **Change of Winds ** from your **Patience** passive & **Dream** from your **Ambition** artifact before playing it so then your **Traffic Light **targets the green card in your hand. **Mines & Hyperlink** decks will be countering your **Traffic Light** as they are both green cards. 

**Tips for the OTK (one turn kill):**
Your opponent should have** 1-4 monsters** when you play down **Casdyne** (short for **Casual Undyne**). Please make opposing monsters **1-5 health** by then. You can buff your **Casdyne** with the **Kr effect from Purple Mascot**, **Shield from Bag of Goodies**, and the **Tea Set from Cracked Table** before attacking. 

This is before you place down **Giga Queen**.
If the monsters have 3 or less health, it is optional to use **Purple Mascot, Gemstones and Prison Bar** to lower them down to 1 health.

This is after you place down **Giga Queen**.
If the monsters have 4 health, use **Giga Glass**. 
If the monsters have 5 health, use **Giga Balls**.

**VERY IMPORTANT: You prefer to have 3 monsters on the opponent side.**

To do the **OTK** (very situational, dependent on opponent's health, and let us say that the monsters have 1 health), total means the max amount of damage you can do without prior buffs: 

A regular **Casdyne** has 8 attack, 9 health and has **Haste**. The casts of **Giga Kick** and **Giga Punch** is targeted on **Casdyne**.

A **Giga Punch** on **Casdyne** makes it a 11 attack monster if there are no other buffs on her. 
Steps when it states **(Alternate Step)** go from 
**3.5** -> **4.5** 
not **3.5** -> 4

Enemy has 1 monster.
1. Play**Casdyne**
2. **Giga Queen**
3.  Attack with **Casdyne** (7 damage) / cast **Giga Kick** (14 damage) / cast **Giga Punch** (10 damage)

Enemy has 2 monsters (Perhaps you played **Receptionist 3** that same turn).
1. Play**Casdyne**
2. **Receptionist 3** (maybe to get the other monster out, would be used if there isn't 2 monsters total)
3. Attack lowest hp enemy monster with your **Casdyne** (7 damage)
**3.5. (Alternate Step)** Play **Giga Queen** to then cast **Giga Punch** (10 damage)
4.  Play **Giga Queen** to cast **Giga Punch** (17 damage total)/ **Giga Kick** (21 damage total)
**4.5. (Alternate Step) Giga Kick** after **Giga Punch**(30 damage total)

Enemy has 3 monsters (Perhaps you played **Receptionist 3** that same turn).
1. Play**Casdyne**
2. **Receptionist 3** (maybe to get the other monster out, would be used if there isn't 3 monsters total)
3. Attack lowest hp enemy monster with your **Casdyne** (7 damage)
4. Play **Giga Queen** to cast **Giga Punch** (17 damage total)
5. Cast **Giga Kick** (37 damage total)

This is without prior buffs, such as **Shield from Bag of Goodies(+1 attack per cast of Shield), Kr from Purple Mascot (+1 attack per monster Casdyne attacks and kills depending on board, max would be +3 attack), and Tea Set from Cracked Table (+1 attack per cast of Tea Set, max 2 attack)**

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
berdly statue ultima

...featuring heal because i am beyond even professional help at this point
----
Step 1) Shit out tokens. Octofriend is good. Recep1 is your earlygame monster that's just sorta existing so you don't mill and you have something to play meaningful octofriends on. Ferry is worse because that 5th atk point matters.

Step 2) Shit out Berdly Statues. If you don't have Berdly Statues, you probably have draw. Draw Berdly Statues to shit out.

Step 3) Win the game.
----
- Heal should mostly be used as a cycle on face and means to stall out a turn or two vs bursty decks or turns 12-15 vs midrange board. That said, this is a 60/40 "mostly", not a 90/10 "mostly", but the situations where you use heal to remove a silence or debuff will come naturally. Don't hold it.

- For people who are unfamiliar with ultima: when it yoinks a card from your hand to shove its insides there instead, the card it yoinked **goes to the top of your deck.** Always. That means you can accurately predict your draw on every odd turn. For example if your turn 2 draw was Maus but it got replaced by Laser Topper, your turn 3 draw is always gonna be Maus. **Use that to your advantage.**

- Since Ultimate Heal can't draw spells, it has a heightened chance of getting you a Cell Key after you've used the Broken Key. Play around that and use your other draw in according order.

- This deck has a deceptively high amount of draw. You can totally draw your deck by t15, but this also means you're not winning any fatigue wars, and this deck doesn't even have Snoelle. Don't be passive vs stall. If you have to be passive, don't ever waste your ex. Hold it till ultima unless you have to prevent something like a Snowgrave.

- You don't have that many spells, and Head Hathy is pretty cheap. Try to play her right after you've used a spell - you can hold the Breaking Love. Note that this doesn't apply to Caged Kings, since they're much more expensive.







Creator: <@519567102781423630>

Some more stuff about the deck:

- You can beat GIGA Queen via Toriel + Round Devices next turn if they waste their glass. It's hilarious.

- If you don't have something from this deck, the replacement is always Susie.

- This deck is prone to self-locking in the earlygame. Spread your tentacles evenly to make sure your board actually has somewhat meaningful damage and you don't end up with Maus-Boxed Body-Left Tentacle-Mobility Tracks.

- You can use Round Devices + Test of Will on boards that are normally immune to it. You can also use the inverse combination and a bit of other damage to set GIGA Queen to 1 ATK if you didn't draw Toriel.
Also, start going face whenever given the opportunity around turn 11. Most players will give you this opportunity. Good players, if they do, will try to facerace you, but then you show them Heal and they wonder what mental asylum you crawled out of before you blast them 12 to 0 with gambit

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
"Totem-for-hire"

Played from onyx 4 - 2205 Legend

75%~ Win Rate, due to learning and Rpaps being a generally bad MU.



The plan: 

Play for integ passive early with Tree and Swatch/Mouth, then start to do more generic totem things.

When we draw a boolshelf,  get Merchire. (Unless you need a specific spell for MUs)

7/4/6 isnt good, but its not nearly as bad as 3/6. Also, t3 BM/Swatch -> t4 Shelf/Cop is a solid curve starter and can get you a smidge of early game bank off passive.

Get your totem ready with 13g, play totem, play your free 7, then merc hire! 

Congrats, you just got an entire solidly stated board for 13g and are 6/7th the way to your 2nd totem to do it again!

This is mostly a meme deck thats been forced to be good, dont expect insane results.

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
skt-whimsalot-pod

The overall record of this is 26-2, though the losses to smorc DT were before I added regular spiders. The matchup should be 70/30 now.

Played to legend. Mulligan varies between games but generally this deck prefers to keep Whimsalot in its starting hand almost always.

This deck actually has a high skill requirement. You'll probably see what I mean. Though, of course, it can also just respond to a midrange play with unanswerable Shambling Mass, because that card isn't stupid at all.

Be aware that TNT man deals its damage to you first. Also be aware that TNT man set to 8 HP can trigger off itself if it kills a 4 HP target.

decks.author: nebularily
decks.notes:
enby-maso-dt
``

20-4 from emerald to high ruby (just hit top 50 :3, stuff after dogday is on most current build)
you play big stats early (viroviro is overstat with any amount of procs, dont sleep on it) then convert that into an advantage when you have low health, using cracked table heals to push you up to stable levels when necessary and using it to push tempo when able
casdyne applies a lot of pressure when they cant remove it easily (eg against skt whimsalot pod), but also works as burst to finish off
use last dream as full heal mainly so you can use more maso and make opponent cry, but getting removal value or using it to get lethal via board buff is not a bad play either
if against aggro do not play chaos blaster unless it lets you play a same turn last dream
loox is here since it can be be 1/2/2, 1g deal 1, 1/1/2 viro +1/+1, or get you that 1 dmg you need to cross a susie plush/ambyu threshold or get to last dream
thinking of running 2 of chaos blaster instead of 3 but im not sure what to sub in atm and it works quite well regardless

lost one time running 3x viroviro (2x is just right for the maso you have) and didnt actually flood board with stats early like a dumdum
lost another time against aggro and i played into it with chaos blaster like a dumdum
another loss was to skt whimsalot pod (feat bomba) but i beat it the next time i faced it cuz i got last dream proc off and cleared 2nd life by keeping up pressure with big stats
the fourth i have no idea and its not on history

decks.author: virtualshadow.
decks.notes:
Paralysis Demon Deck.
This deck consists on doing your best to pursue and find the spell 'self Reflection' as well as paralyzing.

The objective is to paralyze all monsters to wipe out the board, if they are unwilling to put cards on the board
you can force them by using recepcionist 3.

While Burgerpants may sound counter-intuitive is quite useful for getting gold, and well, while they may
have the gold is actually useful for you, as you can use Card Master.

Another important thing is card advantage, while you can focus on damage, is better to try to reduce the size
of his hand as much as possible, as well as use 'Dream' to get two cards immediately when you lose all cards
of yours.

Finally, there is the Angel of Death.
This one is not that important and I think if I had Omega Flowey I would replace him.

But still, is quite useful just one Big strong card as your backup plan, in case your opponent's HP
is not depleted.



Wheelvil is also there because I like him. (Also I forgot I am on Ruby 1.)

Yeah I get that

Since I've been less than nice to the deck, I'll explain what's actually wrong with it.

1) Not having *any* reactive tools except True Self does mean that your whole deck is reliant on drawing a 2-of early on. If your opponent plays, idk, a Great Door, and after random paralyze turn you still don't draw a Self Reflection (it's either turn 3 or turn 4 btw), you explode.

2) Burgerpants played above ruby = you explode. No explanation required. You just do.

3) This deck is very defensive (AoD and two Cauldrons is the only source of offensive pressure) but it stalls into nothing. Even if everything goes well and you get infinite gold and turns, there's nothing to show for it. In mid/high ladder, people will recognize this and, if deck permits, will simply turtle up because the deck does *nothing*.

4) An extension of the previous point, there is no way for this deck to beat stall. Even if you get very lucky receps, stall simply runs more than 2 cards that let it effortlessly kill you.

5) There isn't a single anti aggro/anti zoo card in this deck.

I could go on.

Snoelle would actually fix the control matchup SOMEWHAT. Removing Sushipants would also free up deck slots for something that gives you a fighting chance vs decks that throw everything in the general direction of your face.

Now, as for the second point, even I have no clue how to make burgerpants salvageable here. It's one of the worst cards in the game.

Also: OF here would be even worse than AoD, just because AoD can sometimes win games by itself, while OF can't. And in this deck, AoD is forced to win games by itself.

decks.author: chalk91
decks.notes:
bomba-gems-edition


played from gold to onyx III, could easily go higher but im lazy rn.
the games recorded here are from ruby 4 and onwards (its also where i started making changes to the deck + taking it more seriously)

this deck actually requires some skill, specifically when it comes to finding lethals. unironically 30% of my loses (including the 2 non integ loses in the screenshot) were from me missing lethal. though if a dumbass like me can play this deck and reach top 16, you almost definitely can play it better than me

""bad"" matchups are anything with a lot of healing like rpaps and stuff like that (the 2 integ players i lost to ran rpaps). You can win against these matchups just with a really good bomba chain and if you don't suck though.

some notes:
- clover can be replaced by heroine
- mettabot is unironically your most important card, its usually how you trigger gbc or bomba or even clover
- bomba can fully counter merchire if theres a 4 hp card on your board
- jevil is *probably* unoptimal i just added him because i keep botdecking 3x bag against aggro and i malded
- always observe the board and your cards carefully. Lethal can be hidden in plainsight!
- 2x berdly statue is PROBABLY more optimal than 2x gamer but i hate berdly statue
- shoutouts to mercury for telling me to add fortune tellers and policeblooks this shit slaps 🔥

decks.author: jamarcusjr.
decks.notes:
Caretaker-PV (Ruby III -> Onyx III [Top 10] )

(Names based on "Everywhere at the End of Time" by The Caretaker.
Memory Keeper -> Worsening)

Fun deck, unoptimal but the raw tempo this deck can make is rather astonishing.

Ralsei Dummy had felt alot better being a 2/3/4 taunt and at times a 2/4/7 taunt.

Dalv also feels really nice, a card I never got to use alot before this season but glad to know this deck is perfect for him.

Garbage is the most questionable option but I do think Worsening is simply good enough and allows games where you don't draw enough spells to not such. Could maybe run Dumpsters but I like this better.

Skris, it's a card with good stats, that's kind of it. The gerson addition helps the shock theme regardless so adding skris has been fine, allows for pretty controlled triggers.

Only loss due to the awesome draw of 3x memory keeper 2x Ralsei Dummy 2x stick man skris muffet 2x mascot and no spells.

Early Season Deck

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Omega Flowey DT
(aka green flower dt 3.0 in disquise)

Played to legend
screenshot says 27/11 but in reality it is a lot better (this deck went through A LOT of variations lol), just don't know the exact amount as i played on multiple accounts at multiple different times, winrate is approximately 85% tho

Asgore's truck > trade > keep 1 green flower in hand
Green flower > Girlboss cactus / Christmas tree

The goal of the deck is to clear the board with cheap removal from your 9/7/9 chara and royal dt to make way for omega flowey to run away with the game. Snoelle is a secondary wincon but only go for it vs control, otherwise just use its paralyze to help with setting up gbc.

Some notes:
Christmas tree is kind of insane earlygame, mulligan for it when facing anything remotely fast.
Ambition is required both as a repositioning tool and a mill prevention tool, take advantage of that whenever possible
loren and ralsei are this decks interchangeable slots, preferably you want something that is both cheap and can help earlygame
Mulligan is completely dependent on matchup but pretty intuitive

7.5/10 braincells required, this really isnt easy to play

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
Elite-Shelf-Teg 
(Onyx IV - Master III | 15-5 | 75% WR)

An old deck I always like to make when im bored, this is Shelf-Teg. Its a weird Midrange Control deck, using early game tempo from Police/Stickman/Snake Miner/Poli Bear with the combination of Elite Soldiers and other good cards to try and out tempo early game, and out stall late game.

Integ Bookshelf is the name sake due to the fact it has great options for most situations. Silly Strings for Stall, Merc Hire for Mirrors, and Fortune for if you want to play a 3/4/6 with passive or just need a card out of hand. The reason the deck is built so low to the ground compared to other decks like this is so you can nearly always get Shelf from Elite Soldiers, allowing for better stall and 2/5/7s! (it works trust me....)

Play an early drop so you get T4 Elite Soldiers, mabye get a 3/7/4 for your Poli Bear, who knows.

Pretty fun deck, mostly a pet card deck. Play at your own risk, I know people will play this wrong as say bad deck <:hue:1137287556757721178>

decks.notes:
buffness

played it to legend
91% winrate (32/3) (losses were mainly due to misplays😔)

deck guide: mulligan to at least 1 fan rudinn/spider/sans dog, keep in mind that you HAVE TO play at least 2 cards until turn 4 to get the second recruit, so pay attention to your first recruit options, (in case your recruit drop is bad or not good enough for the first play, bloody tree and flying heads are there to save you!)

play mid drop ---> fan rudinn🤯🤯🤯

you can switch biker lancer and teacher toriel for 2 big mouths if youre struggling against aggro

pay attention to the funny (and unexpexted) lethals that may appear during the games... (it happened a lot)

very simple and funny deck!!!

Having tried bounty, it is pretty hard to actually use if you did have 3x.

You cannot use 2x bounty in a row(or you get waaaay less value cuz wanted doesnt stack).
You cannot run too many removal spells or else it makes having 4 monsters in hand harder.
You need 4+ monster in hand(and to actually get true "value" off bounty you need to have summoned 2 or more of those buffed monsters)

I think it is best running 1x of it, maybe 2 if you have a lot of haste.

Overall bounty is like Stickman, but stickman is more versatile(doesn't lead to understat if you don't kill the monster) and stickman actually helps you kill the targeted unit...

The reward(of stat per G sunk) of bounty IS better though so it rly is a sidegrade... It'd be cool if bounty did buff more cards though.

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
coffinteg
13-2, played to onyx 1 season start
i had a game with sktima that was likely to be lost (but idk for sure) but it softlocked. was before i decided to run 2x lasso though

generally this deck tends to fold to either burn-y strategies or a bad hand. (actually, both losses here are to a chujin, so keep that in mind.) i generally would advise not playing coffin without a way to dupe or kill it, because most decks have silence or some other tech, and it will entirely remove your big guy from the dustpile. however, if you are facing a deck you know wont have an answer, go ham

beyond combo management, the rest of this deck is just good tempo plays and resource management

and remember that coffin doesnt have non-DT so it CAN target chara >:). other important notes: chujin tombstone only takes non-generated monsters out of dustpile, but it does remove them, so it can clear your dustpile for another round of coffins. coffin also only targets distinct non-coffin monsters, and it can target generated ones, too

actually in retrospect, maybe this deck should run an investment or two. feel free to slot something out for it, and if you find success with that, tell me which cards you ended up removing

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
Father-Daughter-Bonding 
(Onyx 4 -> Master 4 (5th Place) | 15/5 (realistically 17/3) | 75*% WR)

I fucking love losing 2 entire ranks to losing 2 games in a row to random piles drawing perfectly.

Deck is fun, the best opener you can usually get is t3 Blitz Quiz, t4 Catty's Dad -> Worm Jar -> vase Breaker -> Grillby.

Aside from that its just a fun sticky tempo deck, play board, react to stuff, get insane G buy back from Cattys Dad.

Lost to Cjester (Unfun but fair, rng is a bitch), 

Lost to fucking Samurai + Prison Bar + Gamer, but not from that, it was due to them getting every board wipe in their deck right on time, 3 times in a row. 

Then, oh man, Lib pile, t3 Rouxls, t5 Gerson tomb for Rouxls, fucking Wereform Rouxls, then Silly Strings on my Pkris, into fucking Papyrus + Pkris to board wipe me, they had 3 cards in hand at this point. I had 6 cards in deck, 0 cattys, no Dustchest, no Hero, and no Tem when they had 2 HP. One of the most frustrating games of UC I have played in ages that I should not have lost.

Deck is likely not optimal, it is fun tho, I am so upset this had 3 loses back to back to horrible RNG, should be 17/3. Hit half way up Master 3 then lost 2 entire bars.

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
Tree-Trust-Tempo
(Master 4 -> Legend 2217 (2nd Place) | 24/7 | 77.5% WR)

This was to test if Vending Machine and Date Alphys Buffs were important, they were.

Most of the time Date Alphys felt alright, it is from when I wanted to add Pezza Time for Vines, but no.

Play Zoo Plant stuff (I forgot Grillby), then play Vines/Flower Fingers + Chips to get a lot of good cheap tempo.

Deck folds hard to Jerry, that card is... I hate Jerry...

 Some slower decks can obv kill you, you are trying to be a mid-range tempo deck, and they eat those.

Best opener I think is either Flower Can + Tree t3, or Asgore's Truck + 2x Green Flower + Vending Machine.

Plant-Totem is a deck I want to make, so I think this was me testing if Plants are even playable in the first place, yeah, they are.

decks.author: iamoseedee
decks.notes:
Name: kbo-chargestice

Description: This iteration of Aggro Justice notably excludes Temmie Eggs, Vasebreakers, Small Birds, and Ceroba. It alternatively has Flies, Headshots, and most importantly: Ball Dancers. With a greater than 70% WR according to the most recent record of 14 wins out of 19 games, I woke up a lowly DIAMOND but will sleep a LEGEND.

Gameplan:
- Ideally, mulligan anything with Charge to play Ball Dancer(s) ASAP. It is possible to play one on T2 (2+1+2) or two by T4 (1+1+2+3+4). Remember that Ball Dancers also buff other Ball Dancers, so playing the first two would require a total of 11G.
- Be mindful of when you might need that extra DMG or G. Consider whether using multiple Duals or Blitz Quizzes now will save the pricier Silence of Headshot for when you need it more, for example.
- Treat the endgame as an OTK. You likely have a larger HP pool, maybe one or two Flying Heads with Taunt, or unused spells. These will allow you to save more of your Gold for Chujin and any monsters in your hand to secure the kill without worrying about drawing a Temmie Egg or Vasebreaker when you need a Charge monster, more DMG, or empty board slots.

Hub Code: []

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
Ambush Bravery
15-5 played in master/legend elo

the problem with trying to maximize ambush value is that you end up running shitty cards. this is a deck that actually runs good cards so you have something to do before ambush turn. power is huge in this deck because it turns good cards into steroided cards that just win early game. 1x overheat is good cuz 2x tends to brick. rest of the deck is self-explanatory, although remember queen pen is a combo, as well as sad dragon assault.

loss explanations:
snails' toilet cuz i was running prosperity 💀 i soon learned this deck is better fast. i beat them the next game
i was 1 hp off killing an ultima kindness running berdstatue chutomb, oh well, was a very close game

i got sandstormed by acid, into skris overgrowth, which tbh huge respect
burn justice but with mixups like dual and catty that caught me off guard

this deck also has a good matchup into zoo and midrange because of all the anti-zoo and anti-mid cards this deck runs

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
sktima justice
played on leaderboard, 18-2

early game consists of doors, spamequins (dont be afraid to rawdog this card! it trades very well and is like a net 5 6/6 at worst), blitzbreakers, coffee to tech PV / bravery silence or die openers, and jerry to tech... whatever that card techs

midgame consists of tomb + bneo / chara to assert board dominance after playing somewhat subpar stats early

main weakness seems to be fast bravery decks that can put out a lot of evenly distributed stats which chara doesn't counter easily, although the second bravery game i played, i was off lethal by 1 hp, and i think if i played more aggressively i might have won. i also had a close game vs mark (playing, surprise surprise, bravery) i really should have lost

decks.author: icenovice4163
decks.notes:
tri-rare tempo
17-5 from ruby 4 to onyx 4 (top 10)
hadn't read the posting rules yet so i forgot to take a screenshot of the rank before switching decks but i remember reaching 10th place

it's called tri-rare because of the three rare need monsters: sushipants, berdly plush, and first starwalker. i originally made this based on a funny idea i had of fwalker -> dummy x2 -> el bailador. el bailador and madjick are actually really good here and you can close out matches quite abruptly that way, but you can also just farm the longevity (not the spell) of your monsters and you'll probably hold enough tempo to win. i actually started playing this yesterday but the deck changed quite a bit overnight, with me adding scarf mouse, cracked table, and jevil this morning. dummy is your taunt (have faith in the little guy), mettaton is shockingly dangerous with stat buffs (especially at +2/+2), scarf mouse is (obviously) your silence card, sushipants holds bigger monsters in check, madjick is surprisingly good removal, especially with sushipants and buffed dummies, cracked table is an incredible card for tempo, grillby closes 1 dmg gaps while keeping tempo, berdly plush has decent stats while letting you curve out higher-value monsters, alphys... didn't really end up doing anything valuable, bookshelf is a metaphorical swiss army knife with test of skill, soothing, ultimaheal, and especially pie, jevil is mostly useful for diamond chaos and devilsknife, and fwalker is your primary opener. with the buff to 6 hp, fwalker is actually able to somewhat keep tempo now. it's the lowest-tempo card in the deck (aside from alphys), so the buff is a huge boost and was a big part of this deck's success.

Berdly Bravery

Played to early-ish season top 5
15/1 aka ~ 94% wr, only loss to… yeah that guy i got shit on by rng, me, the fucking rage berdly deck couldnt kill nor stop a balldancer for 6 turns.

Yes this is still playable in 2025.
Your gameplan is standard midrange stuff, open the game with berdly or recruit drops, preferably followed by knights shield or rage and play the tempo game into a win. This deck can produce really scary boards really quickly so don't hesitate to step on the gas and scorch the opponent of the face of the earth, just make sure to play around board wipes.

Door isn't the main gameplan here, it's just a bonus and can call in surprise rage/overheat targets. Starlo is arguably mid here, but it pops off often enough that it's worth keeping around, if you don't need it just accel it. I shouldnt have to say this but you can use duck off for 1 duck if the situation calls for it.

decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
***Clover Mines***

idk if this was posted already tbh but i made it myself so why not try:

early game, mid game and late game are all kinda the same:

you use spells and clear boards to destroy opponent with value

evil eye is a great opener, you usually stall until 4th or 5th turn without it

if you're being rushed you want jar of worms in hand, always make sure to play some spells when you predict you'll need jar of worms or s signs next turn

caged kings is best played in late game

gem and head hathy's thing can help with clover (also energizer clover is op combo) 

you have heal potential, so your focus should be on aggroing down the opponent

the combination of mines threat and you constantly attacking face is enough to make them trade into you instead of the other way around.

when you have a perfect clover board and can loop to beginning of rounds, you should do it

casual undyne is for when people with 4 hp chujin and play big boards as a last stand

judgement is used in desperate situations

counters:
chargespam can be a threat
also anything else that brings very many big boards quickly
dies to maxhpness 100% of the time (mic drop)

decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
***MaxHPNess (elite soldiers version)***

15 - 5, played in master to legend (75% win rate)
There are a lot of things to say so lets divide them into sections:

**Early Game**

In early game you want to see the opponents' soul and artifacts and get an idea of wether they're gonna rush you or not. For games against rush, you want to keep any healing, for stall games you can mulligan for other things like chara, loren (for the 3 dmg) or evil eye. 

When you're not being chargespam-rushed, you want to keep chara in hand every time. (Chara has a habit of always botdecking so get used to it not being that present). 

You want to generally start with an innkeeper or evil eye if you have it and have it die by turn 4 so you get it back with elite soldiers (this depends though, if doing that means you're milling it may be best to just not let it die so early).

This deck absolutely crushes any chargespam so if you hate it, you're in luck.

**Mid Game**

The mid game consists on activating your combos. Your combos generally consist of: papyrus + pancakes, some kinda bookshelf combo or even cardmaster if opponent is stalling out. 

Papyrus is a really strong card for this deck, especially when healed by pancakes. 

You also must learn when to just accept you're gonna mill and just save g, milling is going to happen 100% of the time so you have to know what you're saving for before milling.

**Late Game**

You'll reach late game with mostly stall-ish decks, so i threw in a dt extractor (accompanied sometimes by reporter mtt) to help with that. 

The combinations of cardmaster + bookshelf and others will keep you alive.

**Other Tips**

You can also lock opp's board and stall the fuck out, then heal and cardmaster.

G is important af, you can always heal so dont mind taking damage.



creator <@401673707497586688>



Other people alrdy said it's a text wall and I am a fan of being descriptive when sharing a deck but something's here really offer little value to say. I'll point them out.

"In early games you want...you or not" This is basic general knowledge everyone should know. We can assume the user alrdy knows this.

"For games against rush" - shorten to "Vs rush" or just "against rush(,keep any healing)"

"For games against stall"-same idea as above.

"When you're not being charge spam...in hand every time." Able to be shortened, "against non-rush always keep Chara in hand"(though I disagree with this tip in general[a good deck should have other cards you can sink your excess gold into])

"You want to generally....it die so early)." This is also generally general knowledge when using elite soldiers. I don't think it's necessary to mention wanting a t4 e soldier proc.

"The mid game consists on...is stalling out." I know bookshelf DOES have combos like pezza time and all...but you just listed ONE combo(papyrus pie). Card master is not a combo. Bookshelf alone is not really much of a combo. If there's a particular common combo you use with bookshelf(like pezzatime on a muffet) it may be worth mentioning the whole combo. 

"You also must learn when...before milling."
I don't actually have a problem with this sentence. General gameplay wants to avoid mill and you are talking about an aspect more exclusive to your deck. What I don't get is why you are okay milling mid game but don't want to mill early game(from t4 es proc). Es generates you an overstat. I'd rather generate an overstat and mill a card vs not generate an overstat until t8 and mill a random card anyway in mid game. Since you claim milling is going to happen 100% of the time, may as well do it early.

"You'll reach late game with mostly...to help with that."
Delete "so I threw in a", just say something like "dt extractor can help with that."

> i am a fan of being descriptive

while I usually agree with this sentiment, this deck quite literally does not need this. All it needs to say is "spam heals and reactive cards without any thought behind your actions and lose to gq speedrun, double talphys speedrun, and dt stall"

decks.author: bachster2.0
decks.notes:
Solid Dawg 
(Annoying dog, solidity, Berdly statue) 

14-4 (from as far as i can read) 11 most recent games in legend and the rest in master 



The general idea is to combine two strong things: Bravery's overgrowth and token drops; and berdly statue + solidity (and chutomb)

Annoying dog, Assault, overgrowth, knight's shield and Rorrim the early game. 

3 accels is necessary to dig for rorrim, annoying dog or just draw the shields without having to pay 3g, i think with 3 assaults, the draw is rarely ever overwhelming. The deck also has quite a few expensive cards so having accel to erase them is nice. 

Berdly statue + chutomb is the pay out, Ice - E can be both a finisher, or a set up for a finisher turn (the ice can be used to lock an opponent down alongside snoelle so you can restraint them and click face the next turn) 

Omega flowey is a really strong finisher, especially with the ice locking the opponent down. 

Snoelle cuz everything she does is helpful for this deck, the para helps with the aforementioned lock, snowgrave do provide a extra punch to finish an opponent off, ice crystal also has taunt so they become "anti fatigue" in the very end. (oh yeah, and she becomes more consistently drawn after accel and overgrowth runs through most of the deck)

oh yeah, two patience games in the middle cuz i got bored and i wanted something to really kill charge spam 

The worst matchup this deck has faced throughout this season is vet midrange:  vet kindness or vet pv. Spade chain just turn rorrim into a "you wasted 7g" card and generally the deck doesn't have a good tool for picking off 1-2 hp targets after they traded, besides assault or recruit drop. And while ice - e does slow the pace of the match down, as well as the dog residues. I often end up too battered by the time i get to berdly statue and just die (or die before that) 

Spaming dog residue and annoying dog also means the deck dies to tasque singer.

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
skt mold dt

15-0 to top 1

1 copy of CoTG is there because it's a vessel. Play it first lategame to recall 2 Moldstacks with Haste, then play Moldmega, trade the Moldbygg, and kill off the Moldstacks. You now have 2 generated Moldmegas.

Omega Flowey and Clover can be used to substitute Frisk. Angel of Death is actually not recommended here.

When using Resurrection, take into account Moldmega burning some stuff in your Dustpile. Depending on the state of the game, you might wanna use the res either before or after this happens. Usually you wanna use it after, if the choice is present.

WR: 75-90%

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
Power-Totem

(LEGEND 2160~ -> LEGEND 2303 (6th Place, Feb 27th) | 26/3 | ~90% WR)

History has the first 4 games use the current list with Kris, 2x Fortune Tellers. These were traded for Sneo, 2x Forest Worm.

This deck wants to play 2x Totem, 3x Powerhouse, and see if it cant get that slow encroaching Tempo build up at the cost of early game tempo.

Loses were 2 games vs "Its Toilet, but I also lay 4x lasso Tasque Singer twice a game" could have won the last game if my totems were not some of the last cards in my deck, they had toilet, any taunt, Susie, Coffee would have won me a turn to draw 2nd totem, or the game. 

I was trying to play too defensively the first game vs max and then payed the price. Learned to be more tempo forward and to simply play as if they always had Chujin, even though they didn't.

Most notable interactions and tips:

Swatch, if possible hold swatch until you can trigger him off Powerhouse at least once. this can be done on t4, play Powerhouse + Swatch, you spend 10g, and if you have any S-Birds or Pink Lasers, you can usually play one of them as well to avoid milling.

It is ok to sometimes play just a 4/4/7 Pink Laser, even 4/3/7. You might just need the early board control and 3/7 is hell of a rate for 4.

You actually have a lot more Burst then you would think. Totem has an almost 70% chance to give you a card that will give you 3 burst the turn of, or after. I have won some games by taking MTT Reporter knowing I had a Copdyne in my deck to Bounce, and getting the extra burst.

People are very bad at playing around Sneo. It has actually been shocking at how often I see a tempo/midrange deck give me 32 HP and then try and Face Race me.

I wish I didn't need to say this, but keep track of your Totem count (Dustpile and potential on board and in hand, how many turns it will take for them to die, etc.), and your Glam Triggers. These are very important to play around for maximum Tempo.

Deck is very simple at base but has a lot of pilot skill checks for some simple things, 6/10 Braincells needed.

decks.author: Deleted User
decks.notes:
The Dojo Special - 18/25 | 72% Winrate - Played from Master III -> Legend (2125 -> 2205)
(It isn't shown here but a win against Casdyne as well)


Mid-Range Kindness deck that operates using Elite Soldiers to refuel, Elite Soldiers is your friend and don't be afraid to mill / sacrifice your monsters (especially early for the T4 proc) if it means you get valuable monsters back. You'll want to aim to secure the board and play on tempo, remove opponents stuff and get good trades in. If your opponent has a plan to win that you very unlikely can't come back from (Especially if it's soon), make sure to race ASAP but don't completely forget about board. Make sure to aim for the Turn 4 Elite Soldiers proc as much as you can (when it's safe to) as that is a HUGE boon that can mean you just win the game from there. Also make sure to keep the pedal to the metal as slowing down can ensure you fall behind and lose.

I encourage you all to make your own spin on this deck and mix and match, if there are cards you don't like in here, throw in something you do like!


General Mulligan Rules:
**Almost always** keep Spider in your hand for a near guaranteed T4 Elite Soldiers proc
Chargespam - Hard mulligan for Anti-aggro / removal
Aggro/Zoo - Hard mulligan for Counters / keep One 4-6 cost proactive if you already have 2 Counters
Mid-range - Keep One 4-6 cost proactive and 1 removal, 3rd option depends
Control - Keep Two 4-6 cost proactive and find a removal
Stall - Hard mulligan for only 4-6 cost proactive (except Spider)

**__Some recommended cards you can swap in!__**

**Silence / Counters**
2x Memorial Statue - 1x in already - Run two if you REALLY need more silence
1x Jerry - Better generally, not as potent anti-aggro
1x Piranhas - Consistent 4 DMG removal that lives as a 4/4
1x Mister Ruckus - Consistent removal while being a Taunt

**Long Game**
1x Chujin Tombstone - Tested minorly, can help immensely with refueling (if you don't want to use Soulless Kris) and give you an extra push
1x Cyberdly - At max you can get 3 Thrashing Machine which can prove handy for removal / gaining value
1x Lancer - Very potent removal that can get you out of tricky spots, can be hard to find the handspace for both Spades early-game

**Beatstick**
1x Bloody Tree - Classic beater, absurd T2, falls off later in the game
2x Donation Stump - 1x in already - Can't scale like Bloody Tree, instantly useful and falls off less later game
1x Receptionist 1 - Can be EXCELLENT against Justice Passive and generally useful. Fantastic T2 as Player 2 and is great as a 4/6/6

**Tested DT's**
Great - Angel of Death, Omega Flowey
Good - Spamton Neo
Okay - The Heroine
Hit or Miss - Chara

**Not-Tested DT'S**
Potentially Good - Soulless Kris
Very hit or miss - Frisk, Clover, Ultimathrash (just run Papyrus lol)
No - The Vessel, Kris, Zenith Martlet, Giga Queen, Barrier

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
Super-Chujin-64

(Master II -> LEGEND 2253 ((6th Place, March 16th) | 17/3 | 85% WR))

Here it is finally, this deck was done on like, the 3rd, and has had 0 changes from me. 

(Name is based on Super Mario 64 and it's Painting Gimmick)

This is good control integ, that is it. "OMG! Zenith is gonna kill me cuz I'm a slow deck" Control is... not slow, that is stall. This deck can kill people on t8, it want's to kill people on t8, but sometimes cant, so it then shifts to killing on on t26. Zenith either gets ran under, or is forced to play Zenith, at that point you should have Asgore, and then they lose.


Loses:

Bullshit gimmick Integ (Botdecked 3 cards that save me at 10 in deck)
HLBs dead on board with 3 cards in hand (Spamton, Beno, and Sneo) ((Lost to Sneo Misplay mostly, so this is a real loss on Pilot Misuse))
<#1299813663913410610> when I mulligan 2x Painting and drew 3x Painting t3 anyways, and no reactive cards till t8 (Die of tempo loss).

The main "Why the FUCK is painting here?" answer is, it's just a really good control card? 3/4/3, cycle, adds value that I want, and has a combo with Chujin Tombstone.

Speak of, Chujin Tombstone 7x, seems cool right? 

You can play Ctomb for Least untill you run out of paintings, then play it for Highest, get your non-gen Ctomb back, and then play that one for Highest, and get your other non-gen back, and you have now replayed 21 cards of your deck.

Sometimes you just got to say "well, I need healing/value RIGHT NOW, so highest." and that's fine, if you can play painting post Ctomb, do it.

GEN GIFTS IS LEGIT FINE! Like, actually though, but it can totally fucking ruin some turns with self lock, so look out for that, and keep track of numbers!

decks.author: bugrofun
decks.notes:
<#1352018837054296124>
Made by Bugrofun (I'm back baby!)
From Diamond 4 to Legend with  12 winstreak until Master 2, but much rougher from here. Still huge 35-11 winrate overall.
After getting stuck on spider-stall-patience vs all those greedy midrange/control decks with skris/gigaqueen/snoelle/etc. I thought that i want something that can really punish doing nothing on the turns 1-3. And then i remembered about Abstract Art + Lamp + Monsterkid combo, which is giving you one of the most dangerous t3 boards possible. As it turns out, it's still as dangerous as it was, if not more.

Deck code:


Your main goal on this deck is take over the board and keep it yours at all costs. Your monsters are hard to remove and spawn more monsters, even 1/1 tokens can potentially be lethal for opponent, your health is a resource, all for breaking face and maintaining threat. You're using lamps to make sticky bodies, monster kids and grillby to buff those and trash rudinn, call of the grave and sometimes faded memory - to get more monster kids. MUCH more. My record is 13 (thirteen) on Martlet.

Martlet is here as finisher and theoretically replaceable by kris or Omega, but i wouldn't advice those. Keep in mind that dusts are triggering in order from dustpile, so if you have lamps first - you will get buffed snow frisks. usually you want your Trash Rudinns to give you Monster Kids, so you can use Faded memory to remove lamps if situation allows. Also faded memory can be used to reach lethal as 2G +4/+4 across the board, or as extra haste token.

Susie and Ceroba here as comeback and winmore plans respectively. Susie sucks when you're ahead already, but can really help to fight back from behind, and Ceroba is just good sometimes. Both can be replaced by 3rd copies of other cards of by other aggro options if you want (but keep in mind that you don't want Trash Rudinn to give you Temmie).

Screen of deck and current placement below:

Side note: i hate infinitlet as much as you are (although it's not a strong deck imo), but i do think that martlet is really cool in decks like that one

Almost forgot match history. Most of my loses were in master 1, but it still should be above 70% in onyx+. Some of those were due to bs (bravery getting exactly redbird into my first and last monsterkid), some due to my missplays, 1-2 - due to reaaally bad starting hand. Also plant justice is a really bad matchup due to it being "25 really cheap haste/removals" type of deck.

ambush-bravery
18-4 (81%)
from about 2130 to 2261

Basically it's control/heavy midrange deck which utilizes big high ATK monsters and control bravery options + Ambush being cracked there. Keep in mind that on the first few turns playing ambush can mean you're milling yourself for several turns, but usually it's still worth it. Armored Minecart is a nightmare fuel for current meta, full of cheap damage.

This deck might be bad against chargespam, but i didn't encounter one. Also Axis actually has nice usecase here (can be exploded manually with crystal), but if you don't think so - you can replace it with extra copy of knight's shield. Same goes for clover - AoD or even raw ultima are also good (if not better) here. Or you can play without DT card at all, why not. Stall is a bad but definitely beatable matchup - a lot of big bodies and burst are not good for stalls. justice decks, light midranges like skris are food - your stuff is bigger and trades more efficiently + you're not that scared of bryan because of good ol' ~~big hat charles~~ Gilbert.

decks.author: truedregun
decks.notes:
s105 justice deck
played to legend from master, 22-4 (roughly 84% wr)
Some people were confused about how to win with the last deck, so I'm here to provide predicted sources of skill issue:
- Getting understatted cards that don't work with your deck from Mo
- Getting only common packs from Undercardio
- Getting bad cards from skris (example: 7g 12/11)
- Not drawing your 2 card combos
- Not drawing the DT in a timely manner
Here's what you should be doing instead:
- Getting cheap well statted cards with slightly relevant effects from Mo
- Getting at *least* 3 final packs from Undercardio
- Getting good cards from skris (example: Mane Ax)
- 8g placeholder kris
- Just draw your DT
I hope this helps in your journey on playing such a unique and interesting deck in undercards dot net

decks.author: wuvea_
decks.notes:
Breaking-badrier
13-2, played to legend (top 5) 

New barrier buff is pretty huge in integ, the 30/30 also sometimes matter although not frequently

Mulligan for a few spells and efficient removal, usually mettabot factory.if you have mtt factory in hand spam a million spells with it to build up dustpile like crazy and also clear boards on a 4/3/4.This has literally zero board presence, so jar of worms and loren activate really often. 

3x shopping is excess here but if you can't draw one soon enough you run out of hand and it's relatively consistent with 3x investment

Barrier is played around turn 13 or 14,right after doom. If you don't have enough dustpile usually stall longer with grill king asgore since it will fuel fast enough. (Also dont play gking before doom because yeah) 

Golden hit doesn't actually synergize with factory or worn jar, it's just there  since you don't really have a lot of ways to deal with armour or other dmg reduction. 

Dalv doesn't feel that great but it's dt tech, play him the turn before barrier and a spell to get past 5 extra hp. 

Anyways uh have fun flashbanging. ((This entire deck dies to ex)))

decks.author: theludicrousluv
decks.notes:
QUEEN'S LUDICROUS BACKUP ARMY
48-16/3-1 (75% WR) I believe i first started playing this in like ruby 2 or 1 and currently at top 3 (wait wtf) with 2239 elo

"late game" artifact generating alot of monsters at once and having great synergy with giga queen? Close enough, welcome back old ESoldier GQ.

Yeah aside that, this is just justice gq and Reinforcement
Unsure if that makes this less impressive or not because building gq isnt rocket science <:hue:1137287556757721178> 

EITHER WAY, your goal with this deck is to get to like turn 8 or 9 to play GQ (if you can, skip the turn before giga in order to play it and extra stuff like snoelle) to then capitalize off the turn 10 reinforcement proc. DO note that sometimes you might play gq later than it but you should be still solid by then if youre not at a massive disadvantage, because, again, ooga gq. One thing to note is if your first gq dies on like turn 9 and you have 2 cards in your hand youre not gonna be able to get the next detach hands because of reinforcement + draw (ambition)(reinforcement should activate before giga batteries 🗣️ )) so you have to be cautious with your hand space if you want your winning tools.

Mulligan wise, anything except like 3 spiders should be good to go, but id like to go for maus, evil eye, dual/blitz , temmie egg or maybe snoelle and gq)

Thats pretty much it. If youre already familiar and good with GQ you definitely can have success with this deck.

Shoutout to pook for aiding with the deckbuilding

decks.author: bugrofun
decks.notes:
Ok, i heard you like snails? I have some snails! Crispy and spicy ones!
Grilled-Snails-Patience, everyone!

From diamond to legend (how the fuck am i top-7), 37-11 (77% WR)



This deck's concept is easy. Snails are almost as cheap as sparring's mother, comes in big numbers and fall short without any good late game. So let's give them one! In form of t7 Grill King and Seam, because having late game early is actually better for us!
Even after getting legend i'm not sure at all about vessel being here. If you want to pretend to be sane person - use 3rd fridge instead i guess. Or you can go all the way doubling down and replace vessel (and maybe tomb in this case) with BARRIER (I saw similar build with it in integrity today and it might be actually a better idea)

Mulligan away anything more expensive than 2G and pray that they will all be botdecked (or even better - almost botdecked)

While picking grill king from seam 20534 times is an option - don't overlook charge options, spade king for silence, dreemurr for heal in case Grillking is [hyperlink]blocked or even DT extractor if all your resources seems to still not be enough. Also after getting in grill's range you can hold your passive in hand and use it to tutor things from crystal. 1 cost and on topdeck? Nah, draw now and without extra cost!

Shoutout to <@911392208526135347> for giving me idea. Pls, remove snailmobiles from your version, it pains me to watch you ruining tomb like that.

if you think you REALLY need ambush, hard mull for it

Going second is based, in most situations you want to drop ambush and next turn play Action Figures on Arcade Samurai or The Warrior. the stats usually overwhelm the oppt and they die. ye.

Going first isn't what this deck prefers BUT if your oppt does nothing, you can pass and burn Dream for one extra turn for them to waste their moment playing any big drop, as ambush shits on that and you have something left over.

Recruit is a big part of making this deck useful. Picking drops that go with cards in the deck already like Trash Ball and Action Figures. Any 6 ATK drop and overheat/rage.

If you need to use both of your ambushes, do it. Take your oppt to Skris if you end up burning both of them (not like overdraw, but using them well) Skris gives this deck a late game, just push for recruit drops that give tokens like Organ Piano, Stalagmite, and Police Blook.

This deck struggles with zoo. use your overheats at the most devastating moments. ambush the most problematic thing and move on. Get Big Mouth, Pancakes, MTT Fountain, cards that heal or don't like zoo from recruit. other than that this deck kinda doesn't like charge spam but you outpace it if you're smart enough so don't worry about it.

TL, DR: Ambush. Play action figures on a 6/7/8 (Arcade) or  (The Warrior) 6/7/9, win game by clicking face, minecart does 3 face sometimes, USE YOUR RECRUITS WELL., and if that doesnt' work take them to skris and call them cring

Apology to @Editor p for them low rolling 3 times. I did not earn those wins or count them

(also thanks dojo for helping me out on building the deck <3 )

decks.author: jaimeeznuts
decks.notes:
Ratvery-Zoo
Final vers of the deck is in the first SS, it originally ran Undercardio instead of Susie
22-6 (75.8% WR)

Swarm is a fun artifact when paired with Bravery.

The gameplan: Play low cost green monster card + op Strength before t3. The first 3 turns are the most important turns you can take.

Preferred mulligan for this is 2x of Maus Hole, Maus, Temegg, and a Strength. In dire situations you can Assault t1/t2 to apply a bit more pressure and gurantee a Swarm trigger.

After t3 is a breeze; play recruit drops, pressure the opponent, etc. Typical zoo shit, play according to the matchup, don't be stupid. Ketsukane is an insane burst/pressure tool, she's also extremely fun)

Imo this isn't the optimal version of the deck, this is legitimately just me running cards that I personally enjoy and that I find fun. If I were being 100% optimal, I'd prolly replace:

-3x Maus
-2x Mo (yes, this card is absolutely garbage here, this is literally just a "this card is fun to play" moment)
for
+2x Grillby
+3x Spider
or
+1x Strength
+2x Glowing Shroom
+2x Tasque Singer

^ this is to debate, but the deck can struggle if you go into opposing zoo and they kill a t1 maus)

The losses were to Dregun getting Jerry from an early Mo while I had 0 Penetration, to Pook literally just being better than me (aka i had a skill issue), I genuinely forgot what I lost to against Nissan and Neymar, Lucky got a perfect TSinger generation from Reinforcement while I completely missed out on Swarm (I mulliganed badly), and Prevolve was a Skt zoo netdeck that I simply played badly against.

decks.author: ivnb92
decks.notes:
skt-lib-dt
Played to Legend (5 of the games are on onyx alt thanks improved matchmaking quality!)

18-2 but full disclosure I played the last 5 games like ass cause I just wanted to post a deck <:hue:1137287556757721178>

-------------------------------------------------------------

- **Wah! This looks like a fast deck! What do I do against Dunebud!!**

Dunebud is a knowledge check. The deck's weakness is that all cards in it feed into Dunebud, meaning it's often forced to use a Dunebud for removing basically any board. Play this matchup slower, count their gold, and focus on attrition - after all 3 (most of the time, 2) Dunebuds die and you're not a few crystal shards away from losing the deck is all but dead. Keep that in mind and do not greed your cards, even payoff ones like Cyberdly and Clover. Just kill Dunebuds instead.

- **I added (insert dogshit tribe monster here) because Cyberdly!**

Put that away. The only acceptable inclusion here on that basis is Temmie (no, not Temmie Egg). Cyberdly commonly gives 2-3 Feather Storms here and that is absolutely fine. Additionally, Maus Hole, Mo and Reflection very often give you tribe monsters.

- **I mill my cards sometimes!**

And that is fine. Also, you can use Mo on two hand slots in a pinch. Do be aware of the fact he isn't OP anymore though (if you saw me saying he is, my opinion fluctuates on that fact).

- **I don't have Clover!**

The deck also works just as well with The Heroine and marginally less well with Ceroba Ketsukane.

- **Parsnik is worse Ceroba!**

Parsnik costing less means it's more easily playable into TNT Man lethals as well as in earlygame. It is also a Plant.

- **Rorrim is bad!**

Rorrim provides a 2-layered taunt wall for the rare random ahh chargespam, sets up TNT Man procs lategame, has Dealmaker synergy due to not summoning a TOKEN, and is underrated in general.

- **Alphys!!!**

No.

- **Chujin!!!**

No.

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
NCG Zoo DT 2, electric boogaloo

Played to master and current top 2 & 3
26/2 (technically 26/3 but ultracard was a dc), aka ~93% wr, only losses due to highrolls 

It is the uninteractive deck once more, this time featuring dumb broken cards i don't like (mo and clover) 

Your gameplan is to forcefully fill the board and punish the opponent hard for any monsters they left alive during their turn. Note that this does take some positioning and thinking ahead as well as good trading. At any given time half of your hand is probably a swing button so you can be really greedy with skip turns and sacking hp for tempo 

Some specifics: 
Snoelle is purely here because it's the cheapest paralyze and the haste is nice, I have only gone for snowgrave once and didn't even get to use it. 
Use Alphys proactively, on an empty board it's a merchire on command. Also yeah it does disrupt cyberd but no it doesn't matter, you only play it for 2 or even 1 feather storm anyways.
Mo is mo, its really good at pressing your advantage further
You do not get 1 turn final rounds with this deck, Clover is primarily just a fuck you semitaunt with burst attached here 
Pkris is your wincondition

This has some rough matchups but all are very doable. Whimsa dt in particular is terrifying tho, avoid it like the plague.

4/10 braincells required

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
lucky obama patience
18-2 played from onyx to master midseason

gameplan: you have efficient tempo swings and a few good proactive drops. the key to playing this deck well is to be good at midrange

bad matchups: against bravery, you need frozen energy to survive candy corn aggro. against justice, you need to pray they botdeck gamer. (gamer from kindness isn't as bad in my experience.) in general, burn can make you sad, but if you're facing a burn heavy opponent, the key is to just beat them before their wincon goes online; dont worry if they hit your face a bit early, the important thing is to win midgame hard. losses were botdeck of frozen energy against bravery and mines justice getting an early edge so i couldn't win midgame / get to lategame

good matchups: any deck that isn't burn or combo adjacent. your lategame is very good, but probably not good enough to beat out decks like cjester or whatever abomination skt made. you go even with average dt / integ lategame, so to win against them requires good piloting and good skris pulls

decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
Snails with Laxatives: Rush to the Toilet

Played from diamond 4 to legend.

Early game: rush snails, always mull for 1-cost snails and just spam tf out of your opponent.

Mid-game: if rushing works and his HP is diminishing, keep up the pressure and use ceroba/paps if needed.

Late-game: so nothing worked until now huh? the rush was easily countered and opponent's HP is full again. Fret not! The Giant Toilet is here for those kinds of match-ups. 
Keep up good pressure with thunderbolts and trade their monsters, try not to take any damage as you'll most likely take a bit of fatigue before pulling off the toilet.

Lost to:
milling my ceroba,
big pressure late-game,
a faster barrier deck (close one),

This deck may look a bit odd but its extremely consistent, you have a ton of cheap, versatile and hard to deal with tools that allow you to basically shut down the enemy gameplan completely if used well.

Notes:
- You do not have any guaranteed healing, do not spend your health recklessly
- You also do not have a dumb comeback or rng bullshit mechanic, its just pure tempo
- If you have a lib in hand you are completely fine taking mills if it means you gain tempo
- Never accel or food enjoyer a lib, you will regret it. A LOT.
- Burn noelle if needed
- Mio mio and ttori both win games on their own with their hand knowledge and disruption

Only loses to some specific burn decks, all other matchups are equal or winning

7/10 braincells required, you cannot autopilot with this deck

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
balloon bravery
17-3 (85%) in legend

main combo: balloon on an empty hand to get net +2 gold with a 4/4 haste attached (remember that balloon is effectively 5G! or 6 with inflation, that cost me my last game)

generally mulligan for a playable opening hand, definitely keep deflated mascots as they are really good early (vomit them ASAP and protect them for max value) and really bad late (to the point of being accel fodder). keep in mind that maus hole guarantees a 3G balloon turn 3 via bravery passive

this deck plays as slow zoo / fast midrange. against faster decks where you've won on board, don't be afraid to facerace. but also you have almost no healing (except 3 hp from laser topper) so if you're only neutral on board then just defend until balloon. especially on balloon turn you want to explode for as much tempo as possible; keep in mind that a lot of bravery spells require a monster on board or a card in hand, so make sure to manage your hand so you don't brick yourself (i.e. keep a balloon and another monster in preparation for next turn so you can use a spell drawn on that monster)

this deck generally has really good matchups

but it can suffer into specifically hyperaggro like snails (one loss) and control with heavy antizoo and finishing power (two losses). people have started to run martlet as skris tech; don't be afraid to play very hard around martlet if you notice your opponent going for it because 0 cost martlet will be able to beat your skris endgame. also i haven't faced it in this match history but im pretty sure cjester is a bad matchup as well, so pray you avoid that deck lol

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
> None of these cards are in the bible
\- Sktima22

Played in legend, 18-2.
Lost to mid kindness buffs with kris highroll and a worse build that did not include Scissor Dancer.

Yes, this deck has Scissor Dancer. It also has Icemeter but you probably know that already.

Your earlygame heavily relies on matchup knowledge and how much of an asshole you are. Sometimes it's better to start off with Trader Temmie, Maus Hole, or, of course, Icemeter t1-t2. Sometimes it's better to defend, wait for their play, then do a big swing with quizbreaker/icemeter combo.

Maus Hole can give you spells. If it doesn't give you spells, it will probably still give you something reactive. Notably, you **can** take Trophy in this deck if you don't have better stuff to do and/or want more stats, it usually won't fuck up your curve. Inflation/Icemeter is also very scary.

Quizbreaker is self-explanatory and no, it doesn't make this deck cringe, because here it's used to make cool shit.

Head Hathy (and Caged Kings) give you 1G spells to the deck. If you draw Blitz Quiz or Snailmobile late, remember that.

Icemeter is the star of the show and a genuinely good card. You CAN play it alongside a single Blitz Quiz and just have a 3/4/4 Taunt earlygame. That's not bad at all.

Noelle is broken, next card.

Trader Temmie fuels SKris at a very funny speed and in general just synergizes perfectly with everything here.

Snailmobile is the most based draw in the game.

Snowdin Sign and Caged Kings are your proactive mid drops. Caged Kings *can* feel a bit too slow, but I think a lot of matchups would get worse without that extra push. You can get a third Maus Hole and a Mo if you wanna full commit to funny SKris, but I wouldn't.

Scissor Dancer is your answer to random kindness shit, random armors, darkspawn, big bob, and generally annoying fuckers. This card is sad even under ideal conditions... 4 ATK when?

Asgore is cool as fuck and genuinely good here. He also kills shamblings.

👆 Big deck guide above. You can skip to this if you don't wanna read a lot.

decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
Justice Spellspam Surprise

I want to start off by saying i have no idea how this even got to meet the required wr, this is totally just meant for gimmick,,,,,
100% Certified Gimmick deck.
WR: ~ 75% In Legend

Mulligan: keep only spells and dt extractor
Early and mid game: try to find powerhouses, reloads and duals
Fill opponents hand with duals to make him mill, giving you precious early game time.
Once you have enough spells and g in hand, invest that into powerhouse. This way, you play around mtt ex and get your dt ext to 1-2 cost (use reload on powerhouse first, then wait for dt extr if possible, if not just do it cuz there are other powerhouses in deck)
Late game KO combo: now that your dt extractor is 1 cost, fill board with dt ext vessels and get powerful dts in hand all while having 1-cost spells from powerhouses to defend from mtt ex.

Also noelle + reload is an amazing combo.
Enjoy scamming your opponents out of the game and then surprising them with incredible boards =)))

Hard-counters:
Armor decks are the bane of my existence
For darkspawn we have true justice + reload that works wonders but if you dont have that... good luck....

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
22-3 in legend

Main cards:

BTreat/Worsening: Mostly ATK debuffs for your Serpent Titan and Pixel Elnina (more on them later). I don't think I've ever used btreat as a silence.

Bloody Tree: The only proactive drop in the 3-5G range that actually forces the opponent to stop skipping turns and play the fucking game already

Zoot Ralsei: Obviously good with Zoot Kris but this is a perfectly fine standalone card. Though "fine" isn't the right word for a 4G mr elegance with potential positve effects and snowball on a vasebreaker condition. Combos extremely well with Contamination.

Contamination: Your answer to all your problems, particularly including Titan, but sure as hell not limited to it. You got a limited number of them so a lot of your success with this deck depends on how well you judge whether to use one or not.

Weatheduo: So the main weakness of this card is that Pixel Elnina is fragile af. Worsening and Bcup treat fix that and PV passive gives additional HP on top. Don't be afraid to use a debuffing spell to make a 5/3 survive, it's actually worth it (unless it isn't, like don't do this in front of justice tjat has been blizzing quizzing or sth)

Zoot Kris: If you don't actually just plain forget to use your sharpshoots occasionally, you already play this deck better than sktima 22.

AoD: Better titan

Seam: Control tech

Knightdyne: Worth using in PV even for a single spear. 9/6/7 deal 5 is already decent but that 2/3 taunt on top makes it feel like the card is still bullshit.

decks.author: pkmoritz
decks.notes:
Rockstar Kris Pie:

Rockstar Kris with Pie can scale really quickly. In the first few turns you rely on Pixel Weather Duo. Then eventually you try to get a strong Rockstar Kris Up. Most of the time you want to attack with an ally, attack with rockstar kris, use pie on rockstar kris and then attack with another ally. This leaves you with a 8/10 Rockstar Kris and if they don't remove it you often times just win. Traffic Light + Kris + Seam also help out a lot. And Forest Worm is just a good card with pie and good Kris target (not sure if theres something better though).

I am not sure how good this really is but it sure is very fun. I love having big numbers on board. I am very open to suggestions on how to imrpove this.
