decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
5.32 Gimmick Cycle

Gimmick deck, rather easy to reach legend but not suited for top ranks.
Gimmicks include: Jackenstein Vines Cloning, Ramb Plantspam, Clover flower cans, Gachapon.

Chujin tombs ressurecting vending machines or vines, op with jackenstein.
Fr when you draw jackenstein its so much easier to defend in the coming turns.
Keep in hand the popato chisps and vines as much as possible, if you draw jackenstein, 3 vines = free 10/10!!!!

Theoretically, the max vines you can spam with this is 9.
Practically you'll reach a maxxxx of 5 in matches with an avg of 2-3.
Ramb is so funny and works so well with all the green flower spam, which in turn works really well with clover.

Downsides: you only have ruins tree to heal. You may eat control for every lunch but zoo and hyper aggro are difficult match-ups. 9/10 braincells required

Have fun! =)))

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
So let me preface this by saying this deck is legitimately unbeatable if played perfectly.

Let me also say that I do not, in fact, play perfectly in every single game.

This was played to 2322 from 2100. During that climb I lost a grand total of two games with this build. 19-1 on history but the actual winrate is something like 60-2 since I had a 40-ish game ws before I stopped counting/caring.

With all that hype out of the way, your resources *are* limited and you are *not* invincible... though the x1 cloning is there for Butler Ralsei off Seam that can very well *make* you invincible. 

With a proper bralsei setup you can just wall Barrier, Ultima and even SKris. Yes, SKris, you can literally just go face anytime you have any kind of board even if theirs outpaces yours threefold because they have like 20 hp while you have 70, and when I learned that you can just do this that's when I realized this integ is, in fact, bullshit. The other reason this deck is bullshit is that you only need to do this in like 30% of your games.

**Hardmulligan for your draw**. This is a miracle deck disguised as a midrange deck disguised as a control deck. Not even for Fortune, that's your second priority here. I know Fortune t2 is every integ player's wet dream, but think of it this way: not drawing your Fortunes is **not** a loss condition, since this is not exactly an earlygame shit-out-stats deck, but not having your draw **is** a loss condition.

There is a *lot* of other nuance and interactions here. I spent basically the whole season and like 500 cumulative legend elo perfecting, and I do mean *perfecting* this deck, and in the process also perfecting my gameplay as this deck. I will say my only 2 losses were to my mistakes when I was literally just rushing through a game clicking green cards due to irl circumstances.

decks.author: pkmoritz
decks.notes:
Arcade Machine TAlphys:

With Kris and Ambition buffed, I really wanted to try building this. Here is what I came up with. It's obviously inconsistent and feels a bit like a meme but I think its really fun. Feel free to give any advice on how to improve this. Ik I'm not the greatest deck builder.

Anyways, how to Play:
The star of the show is Arcade Machine. With both patience and ambition it is surprisingly doable to get it up. If possible, use it while Kris is in hand to make Kris have cost reduction. Don't be to greedy with it. Even with the perfect cards in hand for Arcade Machine it can still be more important to react to your opponent first if they pressure you. By turn 10 you usually should have played at least one arcade machine but it doesnt have to be much earlier. 

For the endgame there is snowgrave and teacher alphys + ice replica. If possible try and use your snoelle early. You can get snowgrave up quite quickly because of patience and Ambition. If the opponent prevents 2 of your lost souls/fox head from entering your dustpile, consider killing snoelle to still get 6 chalks if its a slower game.

You only have one oasis, use it wisely. Since Arcade Machine lets you vomit out your entire hand quickly, you almost always want to use it as a handfill once your hand is pretty small.

Melt is crazy good. Its super strong with cybershelf and dancer. It's also really nice with arcade machine. Since arcade machine will make it cost 0 you can gain some board control while playing arcade machine.

This deck does require some brain cells to play. You should constantly think about rarities. Things like tiny tornado and especially cybershelf give you some control over the rarities in your hand.

decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
Gachapon Gaming Show

Lanino and Elnina go on honeymoon to Tenna's marvelous Arcade!

Hard Mull for arcade machine, if you get it early you'll never regret it

TLDR: play for arcade machine when in hand, gachapon dt and skris are finishers

How to play:
When arcade machine is in hand: try really hard to play only the rarities you have dupes of in your hand, plan for the next turns and take into account the gachapon rarity and dream blocking one draw!
When arcade machine isn't in hand: play normally, don't take rarities into account too much, you don't want to play badly just for the chance of drawing the arcade machine.

On select occasions, you will have a chance to plan for arcade activation turn 5. When this happens, you gotta remember that gacha gives you common, if you already have common you dont have to play the ball! even overdraw if you have the perfect hand but need just a bit more money, arcade early game on full hand is incredible value!

Late game finishers are skris and gachapon legendary and dt rarity.
Other tips:
Don't greed arcade, if you can play it even on 3 cards, it's worth it.
If arcade botdecked, focus on surviving rather than attacking, once you draw it and activate it, you can switch gears. Asdree is also here to allow for easier switching of gears.

Explodes to:
not caring for arcade and letting it brick you
being so bricked early game you have no plays (only happened once) (i added weather duo since then and that wasnt an issue anymore)

Enjoy the arcade games!

Credit to @nightfalcon for helping me with ideas!

decks.notes:
Soul-Absorbing Genocide

Absorbing the souls of your fallen enemies has never been easier!

Stall deck, focus on surviving the opponent's blows and look for cardmaster opportunities, from 6g+ you should play it, 9g+ is already godly value.
Be careful with using your knives! You don't want to run out of max hp after all. If you throw them around carelessly every other turn, you WILL lose.

Multiple counters present here:
Against fast aggro: Absorbax and healing solos.
Against skris: Martlet and the occasional knife.
Against control: DT extractor and cardmasters.
Against barrier: A lot of max hp.

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Ultima Control Patience

Played from master to legend, will play further but queue is dead lmao
23/3, 2 losses due to skill issue, 1 loss to generated asriel on a fully paralyzed board 🙂 

This deck is extremely hard to play against at any stage of the game:
Earlygame lamp, crystal cheese, swarm and cold winter hard-wall basically anything
Mid game your 5 broken token generators become playable as well as stuff like susie and starlo
Late game is gambit talphys, very few decks are able to outlast that

hardmulligan for lamp and crystal cheese to proc swarm, otherwise mulligan for cold winter, consider keeping loren against anything fast

You can get to gambit without draw but its a lot more comfortable with some from either cold winter or starlo, you can be very aggressive with cold winter in this deck btw, you will generally start drawing your discounted botdeck by turn 10-12 or so if you do

Don't forget to play an asgores truck, it's not always preferred but otherwise you cant both play white cloak comfortably and still proc talp (truck is probably the weakest card in the deck but its necessary)

this deck is basically 20 s tier cards in a trench coat so its not uncommon for you to already be winning by gambit turns

thanks to lucky for some help optimizing

decks.notes:
I Can't Believe It's Not M*rio!!! 
(Wario Justice)
(3.32 skris justice aggro dealmaker libslop)

this is, after a bunch of revising and (to be honest) tips from players about what to add to my deck, an skris deck that i (level 98) (in legend) (second ever season) (11 win streak) managed to succeed with, i do not remember all of the people that helped me but i would shout them out if i did

my deck has been doing this general thing for the whole season but this exact revision was only here for like A+ or S- to Legend
15/5, i could probably have a better ratio seeing as i also have an 11 win streak but liek..idont wanna....

this deck has 2 very, very important things
a really strong, aggressive earlygame with cheap creature spam + dealmakers
a really strong, aggressive lategame with cyberdly/skris

and i feel like both of these bleed into having an aggressive midgame, seeing as the "lategame" is around turn 10 when you can get Dark World up
not to mention cyberdly. feather storm is an extremely good card and you can easily have like 3-4 of them when it's time to play him

earlygame is what you'd expect, t1 temmie or t2 bloody tree/blancer is probably what you want most, play lib to refresh your hand once you play all of your cheap ass cards to get even MORE cheap ass cards, and just do a whole lot of playing good tempo cards

play Ceroba on their almost empty boards to piss them off a little and potentially get free face damage (you get even more face damage if they dont play anything which is surprisingly common), play the reflection for free skris procs and to steal their board (extra funny against other lib decks), oh and make them burn cards with sans dog lol

lategame is skris/cyberdly
i'd say this deck doesn't require much of a brain but i could be wrong

decks.author: pkmoritz
decks.notes:
Rockstar Ralsei Justice:

Rockstar Ralsei is a very funny card and I really wanted to make a deck using them + Rabbick. Ideas on how to improve this are as always appreciated a lot cause I am not the greatest deck builder. Been playing something like this all season and fun fact, I now have the highest Rockstar Ralsei Friendship, yippie.

Anyways, how to Play: 
This deck has a lot of very small haste monsters (spiders, clean rabbicks, mettabots, etc). This makes it really great at triggering support effects like orange laser and Rockstar Ralsei.
Since this has 3x Rabbick you usually want 5 or less cards in hand so you have space for clean Rabbick. Dream will mostly be used on Rabbick, this lets you trigger the turbo again and once you get the Rabbick back with reduced cost it will feel a lot better to play it.
For the early game you have orange laser + haste monsters, Pixel Susie stuff, Blitz Breaker and once you have some monsters Grillbys. 
Ramb is kinda insane here imo. Odd controller can go up to like 5 sometimes which is insane. Even if you only get it to 3 tough, i feel like it can be totally decent. It obviously depends on mu and some other stuff but I usually try to get out Ramb asap.
Clover can also just win you games on the spot somtimes. This deck has a lot of ping so clover can get a lot of hits when played.
Burning Snail, Temmie and Ponman are primarly here because of Cyberdly. With also Ramb and Spider you can get 5 feather storms which is really nice. Especially against slower opponents the feather storm burst damage is huge. 
Playing Susie on a Mettabot turn can also go crazy. You can basically boost all of your monsters while debuffing an enemy monster. Susie is obviously also a really good Kris counter.
Rockstar Ralsei is very important here cause its by far your best way to draw. Try saving up some cheap haste monsters so Rockstar Ralsei will never be useless. Often times you will draw more cheap haste monsters with Rockstar Ralsei, which then allows you to draw even more.

<@589544205181911042> ^

Kris and Seam are by far your most valuble cards. If I see them in my opening hand I usually keep them. Other than that I try to get a Traffic Light.

Essentially your goal in the early game is getting up traffic lights while having Token cards in hand so you dont lose tempo. Cursor and Zephyr are your targets for this. Purple Mascot, Spider and Cold Winter help a lot against more aggressive opponents.

Once you got traffic lights up you can play seam and then Kris. This doesnt have to be in the same turn. Once Kris is up you want to really pressure your opponent. Usually pick Casdyne with Kris on the field to get 2 Cost reduced and Stat Buffed Casdynes which is usually enough pressure to win. Depending on situation and matchup sometimes you wana play Kris early after only 1 traffic light, sometimes you wana wait for all 3

Rockstar Kris can obviously only be played somewhat later into the game as they arent that cheap. I usually like to play them right before or after playing Kris to pressure even more. They can also be used when you really need some high atk reactivity. Theyre especially good with frozen energy and during mettabot turns. When you dont have seam in hand but really need to play Kris this is also what you hope to draw with kris.

Some Matchup specific stuff:
- Against very aggressive stuff feel free to use shadow crystals early for things like buttsei. Also feel free to use Kris early without Seam. Purple Mascot is really important in this mu
- Against very slow stuff: If you struggle with pressuring the opponent, try saving up some gold, go for tripple tlights and get 1 or 2 DT Extractors from Shadow Crystals. If one Dt extractor gives you Soulles kris, I reccomend getting el undercardio with the second one

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
Librarian perseverance

Played to early season top 10 (low master) twice 
36/6, aka 84% wr, half the losses due to missplays, other half to “hehehehaw t4 titan” and similar

Very simple stock pv deck with some meta specific picks and 1x trolley problem. 
With plenty of cheap cards to manipulate enemy atk/hp you almost always trade efficiently and can create really hard to deal with boards.

Proceed is kinda forced to be here to deal with random 2/8/14 pillars that automatically spawn in kris players hands on turn 7, arcade samurai is here for similar reasons, its the cheapest card that can deal with t3 power proc berdplushes not named soul cage and also has quite funny synergy with zootsei. Tasque painting is here because it's broken.

Trolley problem is the worst card in the deck right up until it isn't, then it's the best card in the deck.

This will probably be the only deck posted this season without either reinforcement or kris yes this is a challenge

6/10 braincells required

four patience

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
40-4 90% winrate, though 2 of these games were lost to out of game factors so the real wr is 40-2

This does have a hard matchup into very specific zoo decks with a very good opener and if you don't draw any earlygame which is what happened in the game vs dware. Though I was using a slightly different build there and I think this deck has better odds.

This is Seedlings DT. Unlike your typical seedlings DT, this deck is **slow**. This is because zoo seedlings dt tends to boardlock itself often and because seedlings is a control artifact in disguise. Also the combination of Seedlings + Chara is evil. 

Mulligan for Cuptower in every game. If you find it with Another Chance or Ralsei, even better. Rabbick Hole, Flowey, and even Candle are also valid another chance targets (Candle is very matchup-dependent though). You can also use it on Zoot Ralsei's target if you managed to steal something big. Turning a 3/3 Muffet into a 5/7 one is very funny.

Faun should be mulliganed vs earlygame-centric decks. It is an excellent counter to non-Justice Bloody Tree, as well as Rockman, Crystal Cheese, and enemy Candle. It also helps vs faster zoo decks, often being able to neutralize one card and give you a helpful tool for the other, like Assault or Golden Hit. 

Faun is also here because it often gives you a cheap (1-2 G) monster for free, which can be played when you're a bit ahead on tempo so you can afford it and contribute to Seedlings.

You may fall behind in the earlygame, but in mid stages your lost soul cycle is fast enough to reliably get at least 2 lost paps per game. Your biggest worries are TNT Man and Ultimathrash, the first of which can be neutralized by faceracing blindly and the second by doing the same but also having Chara and Reinforcement to fall back on post-gambit if it ever comes to that. The deck is deceptively tanky in the lategame and can outstall, for example, the entirety of <#1429510357239599325>

decks.author: jaimeeznuts
decks.notes:
hanging-rat-dt
17-3 played in A- to S+

omg rare jaimeepost

guide: mulligan for temmie/hanging plug/tpainting/kris. Obviously change this up depending on the matchup, like against justice temmie will be a lot weaker
Hanging plug can be used on just about ANYTHING that's not johnny maus hole or maus hole drop (and obvious stuff like grillby/ncg/zootsei hit by lostphys). It is really good to greed for hanging plug ncg so if you have an ncg hold out for that combo (unless necessary). On the contrary...do not greed tpainting. Tpainting is still good even with -2 or even just -1 cost. a 3/3/5 haste is still good. Also allows it to be hit by Hanging Plug.

Hanging plug and temmie particularly combo very well cause you can get a ton of face damage alongside decent stats for super cheap (this is even better with NCG). Ngl i would also just avoid hanging plugging a lost soul cause you have limited plugs and resetting charge/haste/trans is much better than simply getting a 3/3.

Gachapon is just good handfill, draw is a bit too slow for this deck but it can work. Reinforcement feels like bait but it could mayyybe work if you try hard enough.

Used to use a version that ran 3x Lamp over Flowey and Temmie...that version was still good but this version is a major improvement.

Your current flex slots are zootsei/ramb, those two just kind of felt Fine in gameplay imo. Kris can be subbed out for either raw Ultima, Clover, Titan, or, actually surprisingly, OF. You almost always have a board with this deck so OFing isn't that hard...though Titan/Ultima is likely better.

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
snoring 4patience
at least ~80% wr over at least 40 games, but the last 20 games are with this specific list (i think), going 18-2, played to top 1

this deck sort of ship-of-theseus'd itself into an entirely different deck over time so it's worth a new post

most of the deck is just typical midrange gamesense and change-of-winds management; do not be afraid to use date alphys for a good tempo trade because she is here for being good, not just for snoring/soul cage combos. generally try to send your topend plus a frozen energy to your botdeck so you have a lategame; also note that drawing your 1 costs with change-of-winds is generally optimal (unless you need to draw your dusty chest / cold winter) because they get less value from being redrawn and are best early

if all goes well, you will draw nearly your entire deck by turn 10 or so. if you're facing a deck that goes wide, definitely try to set up cold winter bullshit to insta-win early, otherwise it's fine to just cash it in for a cycle

note for budget players: the seam / vessel topend is actually kinda optional, you can try running whatever as long as it can close out games efficiently

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Midrange Widow DT

Played to early season top 1 
33/2, aka 93% wr, losses due to terrible play vs wuvea twice, winrate slightly inflated because I should have lost 2 more games but got really lucky.

Yeah so who would have guessed that the artifact that gives you a near infinite supply of 1g deal 2’s and occasional free 4 drops that synergises with some of the best tempo cards in the game is pretty good in midrange? I for one am flabbergasted

Gameplan: you have a lot of sustainability earlygame with the combination of spider donut, lost paps and dt extra life, meaning you get a lot of time to get a ton of value from your artifact by just trading, forcing at worst a neutral position in the midgame. From there you can just go face at all opportunities and simply use your burst options to finish out the game

Some notes: 
Zoot kris kinda just wins the earlygame a lot of the time if you get it down, if you need to its completely fine to mill just to get sharpshoot in hand
Its also completely fine to use libloox as a big vulkin, unless you are against control you often dont need the extra face damage 
Pretty sure this is already widely known at this point but royal dt, even if you only have 14 in dustpile, is amazing

This deck runs 1x first starwalker and gets away with it

Oh also credit to Melody for inspiration

4/10 Braincells required

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
33-2 with me changing the build slightly (3 cards) after the first loss

from S+ to top 1 which at the time is 2118. this does not in fact have a bad matchup into pv specifically, both decks to catch me with a shit hand vs their goldfish just happened to be pv. realistically if you wanna counter this play justice.

I did beat a few more real players further down in history dw

this is just midrange. flowey's 2/2 form can be reset with hanging plug and so can be the 1/1 lost soul from crystal cheese. that said don't be afraid to use hanging plug on, idk, snow frisk or broken pillar if that gives you a good trade.

you do need to decide whether you want to play for gachapon or not. mind your handspace, especially with stuff like asgore truck and pillar being in the deck as well as cchese's lost sans.

one more tip is that cuptower can in fact be used offensively. it is fine to play it as an "oh shit i need a clear" button, but it shines if it leaves a board behind. 

this did beat <#1448073653680869567> netdeckers a lot because seedling eventually fucks up their cold winter and the MOMENT they cannot cw you off the board it is straight up over. my advice vs that is to not be TOO afraid of rockman bullseye. they have fucking **vessel **as their dt, and you scale. as long as they're stuck trading you will eventually just win.

as usual for seedlings deck, tracking your passive procs is IMPERATIVE and if you frequently forget which lost soul it is, your winrate will show. this is because you can insta-trigger a lot of lost soul dusts with seedlings cause they go first.

speaking of lost souls, do not play a green flower if you're getting a lost soul summoned in anyway and don't wanna trigger its dust. basically, do not waste an 1/1. unless you want handspace.

pillar is not here just for hanging buffs use it aggressively

decks.author: pkmoritz
decks.notes:
Cyber Baloon Zoo:

I find new Cyber Baloon very interesting and wanted to build around it. Besides Baloon this is very generic Bravery Zoo. I don't really play much Bravery so if anyone has improvment suggestions feel free to share. 

How to play:
Great Openers are Lamp and Maus. You want to be very mindful of your generated monsters. Keep parachutism turns and bravery passive turns in mind as those are consistent ways to get generated monsters. Usually its smart to keep some generated monsters in hand for easier baloon value and greater overgrowths. Especially green flowers in hand make cyberbaloon a lot less clunky. Make sure to get value out of baloon, if you only pay 2G but also only deal 2 DMG you have not achieved anything. Your recrutiment picks matter a lot so always be super mindful of the board state, gold amounts and your cards in hand when picking something

decks.author: pkmoritz
decks.notes:
Jackenstein-TAlphys 
15-5, played to S+, also it managed to reach T

This deck cycles pretty quickly so its good for anyone who wants to play control without having 20+ turn games. Try to mulligan for cheap stuff like spider, investment, mem statue, crystal cheese, etc so that you can get the turn 5 draw. Though in some matchups its totally fine to miss a draw in my opinion. Jackenstein in integ goes brrr and also pancakes + dark zone is funny. Asgore works really well with Carousel, Prison bars or giga missiles. Jevil is mainly here for draw.

I haven't really played or build controll decks much before so feel free to give advice on how to improve this.

decks.author: in_the_name_of_chara
decks.notes:
sonic integ
oh we… we aren’t doing the mario thing anymore? damn it
Play into integ passive as much as possible, for example turn 2 inflation into turn 3 charles into turn 4 fan rudinn. This will help you generate a shit ton of tempo! We all love tempo
Bad matchups: Red soul aggro, (probably) red soul plants 
I put in prosp specifically to deal with dt aggro and in an ensuing match vs dt aggro I drew all my topend and none of my healing and died anyway, if you feel that you don’t want an entire art slot to tech one mu change it to para or power
If you play into integ for at least 5 out of the first 8 turns you can get skris turn 11

Losses: first poon loss (he was playing <#1472662683060011028>) I lost to skris bottom 5 and if I drew skris at any point in time including the last turn of the game I won, also he got frisk melt turn 4 and played 3 cloning on my sans dog and the game was STILL winnable if I drew skris
the lookk losses were dt zoo, I prob won the second one anyway if I had normal draw but whatever
Second poon loss: triple snoelle topdeck, snowgrave turn 12, I don’t need to say more. insane rng lmfao

Hardmull for arcmac, but keep Pixel Kris if you see it. Play yellow card then 67 green cards. Then play even more green cards because you run 3 different ways to refill your hand

decks.author: swissome
decks.notes:
Gourmet Kindness, from A (Onyx II) rank to T rank (Legend)

The story shown is 16-3, the scribbled game wasnt with the deck. There were other matches tho and I think its like 23-5 or something like that



This deck is like a gourmetized burn deck or something because it has lots of burst while still being able to maintain some board. It doesn’t really counter DTEX cloning but I did win some matches against the deck, it mostly depends on the amount of oasis they get and when they get extractor, as well as your own draws ofc.

The gameplan is pretty simple which is just Deal Face DMG. Flying Heads and Mines Sign are good openers and Great Door helps with boards. Sometimes you will need to skip turns to get the right amount of G to kill your opponent (not necessarily skipping for RK) and I like doing that when the opponent locks themselves with low-mid stat monsters since they deal little damage.

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Chimney Mid kindness

44/10 aka ~81% wr, played to top 10 thrice 

Might as well post this too if everyone else is doing so, just general good midrange with an even matchup into most maus cage variants. The matchup is very volatile tho because you just kinda win if you get early chimneys but lose if you dont (excluding extreme rng cases in either direction), also picking the right kindness passive targets is very important in that matchup specifically because you know exactly what they have in hand most of the game, abuse that.

Probably winning into most other things, sadly forced to run rory knight because it's really good into maus cage but against everything else aod or ketsukane is a lot better.

NOODLES ACTIVATES CHIMNEY CONDITION 

p2 arcsam double chimney + passive proc is probably the biggest scam you can get with this deck, the only card that can reasonably counter it is probably like soul cage

lost a game to lurker because lmao no rory no casdyne no bookshelf no samurai
also played really poorly against skt, should have won those games

4/10 braincells required, arcsam unga bunga, some points for good kindness passive positioning

decks.author: gaspadar_nastolak
decks.notes:
hydro-sixteg, 18 - 4
played from A- to S+
widow is alive and is still a decent artifact
royal loox is still good despite the change
best openings: t3 animals right side, t3 piranhas (except if you're vs justice), t4 rloox + other 6-drop
mo is very good for insta board build for omega flowey if you have extra g
cardmaster goes very hard if your opp dared to skip a turn or just happens to have 6 or 9+ g
other cards are your removal tools to control the board and demolish them with omega flowey whenever they commit a bit less to a board
seam and bookshelves are your techs and lategame (you don't really need a stall-like lategame this season tho <:hue:1137287556757721178> )
another important thing is that you need to choose when to proc widow and when to not in some situations
losses:
reiuji loss was me being dumbass and greeding cardmaster, it was very winnable
gerson fan loss was a highroll from their side and me not knowing whether i should take a mill to gain more g and defend better next turn or to play 1 card now (chose the latter and lost in some turns)
muitodoido loss was me misplaying and taking more damage than necessary which allowed them to finish me early with rk
when playing this, keep in mind that you can tank damage (you have nat heal and totguards) for better plays and combos. don't be afraid to play omega flowey without burst damage, it's alright too and can be used for defense. do everything to control the board because it's a control deck.

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
19-1 from A to legend. The only loss was to me getting pizza, fucking up that turn, and then grossly miscounting my damage on the following one <:hue:1137287556757721178>

AKA hitler patience, aka sequel to <#1410210779596329052> 

Hard mulligan for dunebud. Your ideal opener is dunebud + replica. 

If they don't answer it, you basically win vs anything board-centric but you may get cheesed — if you're really afraid of that your anti cheese options include playing a bag or tall miner to force them to respond, having 2 ice shocks in hand, or using frozen energy to strengthen your board (in order of preference).

If they do answer it, look for a 2g spell off your change of winds — if you find it, you can immediately retaliate with dunebud + 2g spell from hand + cost-reduced 1g spell from topdeck.

That's it. Keep your sweet little bundle of cancer all over the board until you get enough chip damage and gold to simply burn people with gemstones/shards. Don't be afraid of playing miner/bag for tempo but keep track of how many you have left (replica helps).

The biggest skill factors are ordering (which can get complicated here especially since this is a very APMy deck) and hand management. A lot of long-term hand management.

Cooler cooler is your flex slot. Replace it as you will. I chose this card bc it's a budget 4th dunebud (this deck REALLY wants dunebud tho) and if drawn on t2 as p2 vs zoo, with fenergy it can trade into a 1 atk monster and make them wish they played league of legends instead of this game.

Remember that 2-3-4 bags can also output huge damage and it is more gold efficient than miner if you have the spells for it. This will come up rarely, but it will come up.

You can actually run this with no DT. Vessel is not that strong here, though it helps.

decks.author: casdyne
decks.notes:
Electric-Soul
18-4 Played to S (top 10!) from A- (holy shit rubies give no elo)

Wow okay yeah I HATE thorn ring i HATE dune bud I hate Pot Of Greed and finally, I HATE ICE SHOCK.

playing this deck vs. ring:
you are on an 8-9 turn timer. your opponent has no taunt monsters and will likely try to ice shock you early or late to slow you down, keep this in mind and don't play into it. get a full board and cast strengths as soon/wisely as possible, and click face. chances are you will win due to raw damage, and try to keep your board full.

playing this VS PV or Justice
you may not want to play monsters right away. play them maybe when you have access to a full board and no sooner, the rest of the game will depend on what kind of deck they are playing, so be an undercards player.

Playing this VS everything else
keep your board full, force them to trade, if you get behind, play for skris.

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Titan Fuzzy Perseverance

17/3, aka 85% wr, all losses due to missplays, played to top 10

This deck is being posted out of spite, I overhauled this shit like 6 times to get to this version and I want to get it out of my system, that being said: very fun deck

The star of the show is fuzzy, its incredibly versatile earlygame if you have the small spells to fuel it and it can get honestly very stupid amounts of value in the midgame if its allowed to attack multiple times per turn or just grow to become like a 2/7/7, tall miner fuzzy can genuinely sweep just about any board.

The rest of the deck is just here to take advantage of the metric shitload of small ping you have and to fuel skris. Fountain usually doesn't actually come into play that much in most matchups, it's just here to break control 

Ring matchup is even, if you manage to get fuzzy up early they basically cant stop it and you have some other tools as well like a bunch of deal 2, game bomb, trolley problem and tall miner but that matchup is so volatile that it usually just comes down to who gets scammed by draw rng and who doesnt

because its funny: lock matchup is 90/10, yes i lost in the history but thats because i completely lost the plot in that match, all other variants of this deck went undefeated

Sorry for forgetting before, credit to Melody for opop concept

7.5/10 braincells required, if you mess up once you explode

wheelvil was a much later addition. this deck really needs some cheap boardwipe but i got only register to easily trigger Axis with and i don't have *reliable* access to Proceed or Overheat to combo with the 7/7. solution? wheelvil. it's been an alright card so far (it's only been in the last 5 games) but with health art i expect it to shine vs the mid kindness matchup which is the only bad matchup this deck has

everything else is pretty much mandatory though you could experiment with rudolph over 1 hole or even over wheelvil
